tooltip: Factory function that creates a Terminal instance from a backend + optional PTY
Convenience factory for tests — creates a terminal with a backend and default dimensions
Run the same test suite against multiple backends — cross-compatibility testing
High-level API: backend + PTY + search + screenshots + region selectors
Interface that all backends implement — ~18 methods for terminal I/O
Read-only terminal interface — getText, getCell, getCursor, getMode, getScrollback
Single terminal cell with text content, foreground/background colors, and style flags
Cursor position and visibility — row, col, visible, shape
Terminal mode flags — alternate screen, bracketed paste, mouse tracking, etc.
Lazy region selector — recomputes on every access for live terminal state
Single cell with positional context — row, col, fg, bg, bold, italic
A row with positional context — row number and cellAt(col) access
Send raw data to the terminal backend — the core input method for writing escape sequences and text
Launch a process with a PTY — test real CLI apps with stdin/stdout connected to the terminal
Simulate a keypress — arrows, Enter, Ctrl+C, function keys, and modifier combinations
Change terminal dimensions — triggers reflow and SIGWINCH for testing responsive behavior
Gracefully shut down the terminal — kills PTY process, cleans up resources
Search for text in the terminal output — returns position (row, col) of first match or null
Search for all regex matches in terminal output — returns array of positions
Capture terminal state as SVG — text-based, diffable, with colors and styling preserved
Capture terminal state as PNG image — pixel-perfect rendering for visual regression testing
Simulate a mouse click at terminal coordinates — sends mouse tracking escape sequences
Simulate a double-click at terminal coordinates — two clicks with configurable delay
Factory for the xterm.js backend — the most widely used JavaScript terminal emulator
Factory for the vterm.js backend — full-featured TypeScript emulator with 100% terminfo.dev coverage
Factory for the Ghostty WASM backend — GPU-quality terminal emulation in headless mode
Factory for the vt100.js backend — lightweight pure TypeScript VT100/VT220 emulator
Vitest matcher — asserts a terminal region contains the expected text
Vitest matcher — asserts exact text match on a terminal region
Vitest matcher — asserts multi-line content matches expected lines
Vitest matcher — asserts a cell has bold styling
Vitest matcher — asserts a cell has italic styling
Vitest matcher — asserts a cell has the expected foreground color
Vitest matcher — asserts a cell has the expected background color
Vitest matcher — asserts the cursor is at the expected row and column
Vitest matcher — asserts a terminal mode is enabled (e.g., alternate screen)
Vitest matcher — visual regression testing via SVG/PNG screenshot comparison
Vitest matcher — text-based terminal snapshot testing
Vitest matcher — asserts the terminal title matches the expected string
Secondary terminal buffer used by fullscreen apps — preserves main scrollback
Lines that have scrolled above the visible viewport — terminal history
The visible portion of the terminal — rows x cols currently displayed
The active terminal grid — rows x cols of cells with text and attributes
OS-level interface (PTY) that emulates a real terminal — enables running CLI apps in tests
Terminal mode where pasted text is wrapped in escape sequences — prevents paste injection
The WHERE part of Termless — term.screen, term.row(n), term.cell(r,c), term.range(...)
Playwright-style polling — matchers accept { timeout } for automatic retry until assertion passes
Termless views recompute on every access — always reflect current terminal state, never stale
Erase in Display — clear part of the screen (below cursor, above cursor, or entire screen)
Erase entire screen — CSI 2 J clears all visible cells without affecting scrollback
Erase screen + scrollback — CSI 3 J clears visible screen and the entire scrollback buffer
Cursor Position — CSI row;col H moves cursor to an absolute position on screen
Cursor Back — move cursor left N columns
Cursor Forward — move cursor right N columns
Cursor Up — move cursor up N rows
Cursor Down — move cursor down N rows
DEC Set Top and Bottom Margins — defines the scroll region for independent scrolling
DEC Text Cursor Enable Mode — DEC private mode ?25 to show or hide the text cursor
DEC Save Cursor — save cursor position and attributes for later restoration with DECRC
DEC Set Cursor Style — set cursor shape: block, underline, or bar; blinking or steady
Erase in Line — clear part of the current line (right of cursor, left, or entire line)
Insert Character — insert blank characters at cursor, shifting existing characters right
Delete Character — delete characters at cursor, shifting remaining characters left
Insert Lines — insert blank lines at cursor position, pushing existing lines down
Delete Lines — delete lines at cursor position, pulling lines up from below
Scroll Up — scroll the display up N lines, new blank lines at the bottom
Scroll Down — scroll the display down N lines, new blank lines at the top
Reset to Initial State — full terminal reset of cursor, modes, scroll regions, and colors
Primary Device Attributes — query the terminal for its type and supported features
Device Status Report — query the terminal for status, most commonly cursor position (DSR 6)
Clickable hyperlinks in terminal output — text between OSC 8 markers becomes a link
Clipboard access — read/write the system clipboard from terminal applications
Full-featured modern terminal emulator in pure TypeScript — 100% terminfo.dev feature coverage
Popular browser-based terminal emulator — most widely used JavaScript terminal
GPU-accelerated terminal emulator by Mitchell Hashimoto — available as WASM backend
React TUI framework for modern terminal apps — uses Termless for testing
Terminal feature compatibility database — powered by Termless probe results
Modern testing framework powered by Vite — Termless matchers integrate as Vitest custom matchers
Select Graphic Rendition — ANSI escape codes for text styling (bold, colors, underline)
Control Sequence Introducer (ESC[) — prefix for most ANSI terminal commands
Operating System Command — terminal escape sequences for titles, hyperlinks, clipboard
Device Control String — escape sequences for device-specific data (Sixel graphics, DECRQSS)
International standard defining terminal control functions — basis of ANSI escape codes
Modern keyboard input protocol with key release events, modifiers, and Unicode
24-bit RGB color support via ESC[38;2;R;G;Bm — 16 million colors
A character occupying two terminal columns — CJK ideographs and most emoji
What humans see as a single character — may be multiple Unicode codepoints (e.g., emoji with ZWJ)
Buffer all output between start/end markers and paint as one frame — eliminates flicker
Terminal reports mouse clicks, movement, and scroll events to the application
Terminal notifies the app when the window gains or loses focus
TTY mode where every byte is delivered immediately — no echo, no line editing, no signals
Running the same test suite against multiple terminal backends to verify cross-compatibility
Running terminal tests without a visible terminal window — in-process emulation
Visual regression testing via SVG/PNG snapshots of terminal output
Name-based backend resolution system — async loading of backends by string identifier
Compare two terminal buffers cell-by-cell — returns only changed cells for efficient diffing
Record a terminal session — captures all input/output for later replay
Replay a previously recorded terminal session — reproduce interactions deterministically
Deterministic timer for tests — advance time manually, fire callbacks synchronously
